# DepOversight

> Dependency intelligence platform that monitors upstream open-source PRs, commits, issues, releases, changelogs, and advisories to surface security-relevant dependency signals before CVEs, advisories, or scanner alerts exist.

Canonical URL: https://depoversight.com/

## Supported languages

- en (English) — default
- es (Español)
- fr (Français)
- de (Deutsch)
- pt (Português)
- ja (日本語)
- ko (한국어)
- nl (Nederlands)
- it (Italiano)
- pl (Polski)
- sv (Svenska)
- fi (Suomi)

## Key sections

- Hero
- Live demo dashboard
- Security scenarios
- Timeline / exposure window
- Problem
- Solution
- What DepOversight tells you
- How it works
- Detection engine
- Comparison vs CVE scanners
- Integrations
- Who it's for
- Early access form

## What DepOversight does

- Reads upstream activity (PRs, commits, issues, releases, changelogs, advisories) across the declared dependency graph.
- Detects security-relevant patterns: fix language, security language, disclosure language, surface-area changes.
- Tags signals by source type: PR, commit, issue, release, changelog, advisory.
- Tracks release state: merged, released, only on a development branch.
- Surfaces signals to a review queue with sources, evidence, and recommended action.
- Provides a PR check that blocks dependency updates touching flagged versions until reviewed.

## What DepOversight does not claim

- It does not run exploits.
- It does not generate proof-of-concept payloads, reproduction steps, or exploitation instructions.
- It does not claim to detect all vulnerabilities.
- It does not claim guaranteed pre-CVE detection.

## Disclaimer

DepOversight surfaces security-relevant review signals based on public upstream activity. A signal is not a confirmed vulnerability unless linked to an advisory or CVE.

## Early access

Request early access at https://depoversight.com/#early-access
